chunks.text: An Operational Semantics for Hybrid Systems An Operational Semantics for Hybrid Systems Researchers: Haiyang Zheng Advisor: Edward A. Lee This research interprets hybrid system models as executable programs written in a domain-specific programming la

ograms written in a domain-specific programming language that has a hybrid system semantics. The semantic properties of such language affect our ability to understand, execute, and analyze a hybrid system model [3]. We focus on several semantic issue

stem model [3]. We focus on several semantic issues that come in defining such a programming language, such as the interpretations of discrete-event signals and discontinuities in continuous-time signals, and the handling of multiple simultaneous int

als, and the handling of multiple simultaneous interactions between these signals, and the consequences of numerical ODE solver techniques. We describe the solution by giving its operational semantics [2]. We will continue identifying and solving mor

[2]. We will continue identifying and solving more semantics issues and complete the operational semantics of the programing language. We will implement the operational semantics in HyVisual [1]. References: 1. C. Brooks, A. Cataldo, E. A. Lee, J. L

erences: 1. C. Brooks, A. Cataldo, E. A. Lee, J. Liu, X. Liu, S. Neuendorffer, H. Zheng, "HyVisual: A Hybrid System Visual Modeler," Technical Memorandum UCB/ERL M05/ 24, University of California, Berkeley, CA 94720, July 15, 2005. 2. E. A. Lee and H

keley, CA 94720, July 15, 2005. 2. E. A. Lee and H. Zheng, "Operational Semantics of Hybrid Systems," Invited paper in Proceedings of Hybrid Systems: Computation and Control (HSCC) LNCS 3414, Zurich, Switzerland, March 9-11, 2005. 3. A. van der Schaf
